outcome_contract.why: Staff need to record at checkout whether a guest used the fridge, and see that flag on the checkout screen so nothing is missed before the guest leaves.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Staff can toggle a fridge-used flag per guest on the checkout screen; the FRIDGE column shows a fridge icon when the flag is set, so staff know to retrieve fridge items before the guest departs.

engineering_coverage.applicable: `Refrigerator` icon in FRIDGE cell; `Button` with `disabled` prop during write
Empty cell when unset; icon when set; button disabled during write; toast on error
Explicit `fridgeStorage` Firebase rule, role-gated write, null-writes allowed
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No PII; same auth model as all other guest-facing nodes

engineering_coverage.applicable: Schema unit, hook unit, mutation unit, component, integration, rules, parity snapshot
New Zod schema, TS types; `Guest.fridgeUsed?: boolean`; `CheckoutTableProps` updated; `CheckoutRow` unchanged
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Breaking type change on `Guest` — all callsites in scope

engineering_coverage.applicable: Single Firebase `update()` per toggle; bounded node size; disabled button prevents double-fire
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: `finally` block ensures pending set is always cleared

engineering_coverage.applicable: No feature flag; additive Firebase node; revert 5–6 files to rollback
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Firebase node persists harmlessly after rollback

stage_payload.summary: The checkout screen has a FRIDGE column that always shows blank. This plan wires real Firebase data into it: a boolean `{ used: boolean }` flag per occupant at `fridgeStorage/<occupantId>`, displayed as a `Refrigerator` icon when set. Staff can toggle the flag directly on the checkout row. The implementation follows the bag-storage analogue exactly, extended with a direct-write mutation hook, a per-row disabled-during-write pending state, and full coverage of the occupant lifecycle (archive, delete-guest cleanup). Seven tasks, three waves. No feature flags, no migration.
goals: Staff toggle fridge-used per guest row on the checkout screen.
FRIDGE column shows `Refrigerator` icon when `used === true`, blank otherwise.
Fridge flag archived alongside the guest; deleted alongside the guest record.
Firebase security rule explicitly covers `fridgeStorage` node.
non_goals: No free-text, no item list, no POS transaction.
No fridge UI on any screen other than checkout.
assumptions: `Refrigerator` icon confirmed available in lucide-react installed in this app.
selected_approach: Bag-storage pattern for read path; disabled-during-write pending state for toggle UX.
Why planning is not reopening option selection:
Analysis settled all design decisions. Operator confirmed all requirements. No open forks.

task_briefs.title: Update CheckoutTable — type change, Refrigerator icon, toggle button
Wire Checkout.tsx — subscribe, pending state, toggle handler
Archive, delete-guest cleanup, and Firebase rules
Tests — all layers
task_briefs.deliverable: New and updated test files across schema, hooks, mutation, component, integration, rules, parity

areas.label: Fridge flag recording at checkout
areas.trigger: Guest hands fridge items to staff; staff opens checkout screen
areas.flow: 1. FRIDGE column shows toggle button per row. 2. Staff clicks toggle. 3. Button disables (pending state). 4. `useSetFridgeUsedMutation` calls `update(ref(db, fridgeStorage/<occupantId>), { used: true })`. 5. Firebase subscription delivers update → `Refrigerator` icon appears; button re-enables. 6. Clicking again reverses: `used: false`, icon disappears. 7. On error: toast shown; button re-enables; Firebase state unchanged.
areas.seams: Rollback: revert TASK-04 and TASK-05 changes; Firebase node persists harmlessly

areas.label: Guest archival
areas.flow: `fridgeStorage/<occupantId>` read → copied to `archive/fridgeStorage/<occupantId>` → live path nulled. Joins existing bagStorage archival block.

areas.label: Guest deletion
areas.flow: `fridgeStorage/<occupantId> = null` added to the `updates` map alongside `bagStorage/<occupantId> = null` at line 119.

areas.label: Firebase security
areas.flow: Explicit `fridgeStorage` rule: authenticated read for all users; role-gated write (staff/admin/manager/developer/owner) without `newData.exists()` — null writes allowed for archive and delete flows.
areas.seams: Rules change is safe to deploy before or alongside code
